children.heading: .—Contracts and cooperative agreements
children.label: 1 .—Contracts and cooperative agreements
children.text: The Secretary of the Navy may enter into contracts and cooperative agreements with the Naval Academy Athletic Association for the purpose of supporting the athletic and physical fitness programs of the Naval Academy. Notwithstanding , the Secretary may enter such contracts or cooperative agreements on a sole source basis pursuant to . Notwithstanding chapter 63 of title 31, a cooperative agreement under this section may be used to acquire property or services for the direct benefit or use of the Naval Academy.section 3201(e) of this titlesection 3204(a)(5) of this title

children.heading: .—Leases
children.label: 2 .—Leases
children.text: The Secretary may enter into leases, in accordance with , or licenses with the Association for the purpose of supporting the athletic and physical fitness programs of the Naval Academy. Any such lease or license shall be deemed to satisfy the conditions of .section 2667 of this titlesection 2667(h)(2) of this title

children.heading: .—Use of Navy Personal Property by the Association
children.label: b .—Use of Navy Personal Property by the Association
children.text: The Secretary may allow the Association to use, at no cost, personal property of the Department of the Navy to assist the Association in supporting the athletic and physical fitness programs of the Naval Academy.

children.heading: .—Support received from the association
children.label: 1 .—Support received from the association
children.text: Notwithstanding , the Secretary may accept from the Association funds, supplies, and services for the support of the athletic and physical fitness programs of the Naval Academy. For purposes of this section, employees or personnel of the Association may not be considered to be employees of the United States.section 1342 of title 31

children.heading: .—Funds received from ncaa
children.label: 2 .—Funds received from ncaa
children.text: The Secretary may accept funds from the National Collegiate Athletic Association to support the athletic and physical fitness programs of the Naval Academy.

children.heading: .—Limitation
children.label: 3 .—Limitation
children.text: The Secretary shall ensure that contributions under this subsection do not reflect unfavorably on the ability of the Department of the Navy, any of its employees, or any member of the armed forces to carry out any responsibility or duty in a fair and objective manner, or compromise the integrity or appearance of integrity of any program of the Department of the Navy, or any individual involved in such a program.

children.heading: .—Retention and Use of Funds
children.label: d .—Retention and Use of Funds
children.text: Notwithstanding , funds received under this section may be retained for use in support of athletic and physical fitness programs of the Naval Academy and shall remain available until expended.section 2260(d) of this title

children.heading: .—Licensing, marketing, and sponsorship agreements
children.label: 1 .—Licensing, marketing, and sponsorship agreements
children.text: An agreement under subsection (a)(1) may, consistent with sections 2260 (other than subsection (d)) and 8022(b)(3) of this title, authorize the Association to enter into licensing, marketing, and sponsorship agreements relating to trademarks and service marks identifying the Naval Academy, subject to the approval of the Department of the Navy.

children.heading: .—Limitations
children.label: 2 .—Limitations
children.text: No such licensing, marketing, or sponsorship agreement may be entered into if it would reflect unfavorably on the ability of the Department of the Navy, any of its employees, or any member of the armed forces to carry out any responsibility or duty in a fair and objective manner, or if the Secretary determines that the use of the trademark or service mark would compromise the integrity or appearance of integrity of any program of the Department of the Navy, or any individual involved in such a program.

children.heading: .—Service on Association Board of Control
children.label: f .—Service on Association Board of Control
children.text: The Association is a designated entity for which authorization under sections 1033(a) and 1589(a) of this title may be provided.

qualify as a nonprofit organization under section 501(c)(3) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 and operates in accordance with this section, the laws of the State of Maryland, and the constitution and bylaws of the Association; and
operate exclusively to support the athletic and physical fitness programs of the Naval Academy.

children.heading: .—Association Defined
children.label: h .—Association Defined
children.text: In this section, the term “Association” means the Naval Academy Athletic Association.
